--- 
title: "Guide for Wise Investing"
author: "Junghoon Shin"
date: "`r paste(Sys.time(), 'KST')`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
bibliography: [book.bib, packages.bib]
csl: apa-with-abstract.csl
biblio-style: apalike
link-citations: yes
url: 'https\://junghoon-shin.github.io/'
github-repo: "junghoon-shin/guide-for-wise-investing"
cover-image: "images/Warren.jpg"
description: "This is a personal investing guide based on the analysis of company financials, industry and market status, and global economy."
---

# About the author {#author .unnumbered}

Junghoon Shin is a Korean doctor, cancer scientist, and statistical programmer. He earned his MD and MSc from the [Seoul National University College of Medicine](https://medicine.snu.ac.kr/) and is now a PhD student at the Graduate School of Medical Science and Engineering, [Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology](https://www.kaist.ac.kr/en/). His research is primarily focused on the transcriptome dysregulation patterns observed in cancer cells, which have a key role in the cancer development process. Before coming to KAIST, he received the residency training at the Department of Internal Medicine, [Seoul National University Hospital](http://www.snuh.org/global/en/main.do) and earned his medical board certification in 2018. Recently he has had a particular interest in machine learning, data visualization, and obviously, investment. He spends most of his out-of-work time digging out early-stage companies that have a great mission, strength, and fine management.\
\

**Contact the author**\
junghoonshin@kaist.ac.kr\
https://github.com/junghoon-shin

```{r, include = F}
# automatically create a bib database for R packages

knitr::write_bib(c(.packages(), "bookdown", "knitr", "rmarkdown"), "packages.bib")
```

```{r, include = F}
# initialize environment

library(pacman)

p_load(bookdown,
       ComplexHeatmap,
       cowplot,
       DT,
       furrr,
       ggpubr,
       googledrive,
       googlesheets4,
       grid,
       gridExtra,
       httr,
       kableExtra,
       knitr,
       magrittr,
       RColorBrewer,
       scales,
       tidyverse,
       XML)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, out.width = "100%")

theme_update(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, vjust = 0.5))
```

```{r}
# define functions

read_html = function(url, which = integer(), header = T, trim = T) {
  readHTMLTable(rawToChar(GET(url)$content), which = which, header = header, trim = trim)
}

my_datatable = function(tbl, paging = F, scrollY = unit(700, "lines"), caption = NULL) {
  DT::datatable(tbl,
                options = list(paging = paging,
                               searching = T,
                               ordering = T,
                               scrollX = T, 
                               scrollY = scrollY,
                               fixedHeader = T,
                               fixedColumns = T,
                               autoWidth = T,
                               initComplete = JS("function(settings, json) {", 
                                                 "$(this.api().table().header()).css({'font-family': 'Arial', 'font-size': '14px'});",
                                                 "}")),
                extensions = "FixedColumns",
              rownames = F,
              caption = caption) %>%
    formatStyle(columns = 1:ncol(tbl), fontFamily = "Arial", fontSize = "14px")
}
```
